Forecasting On Air Passenger data¶

In [19]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

from statsmodels.tsa.arima.model import ARIMA
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error
In [20]:
df=pd.read_csv(r'C:\Users\anupa\Desktop\Python Learning\Forecasting\AirPassengers.csv')
In [21]:
df.head()
Out[21]:
Month Passengers
0 1949-01 112
1 1949-02 118
2 1949-03 132
3 1949-04 129
4 1949-05 121
In [22]:
df.tail()
Out[22]:
Month Passengers
139 1960-08 606
140 1960-09 508
141 1960-10 461

143 1960-12 432

df.info()

In [26]:
df['Month'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Month'])
In [27]:
df.info()

<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>
RangeIndex: 144 entries, 0 to 143
Data columns (total 2 columns):
 #   Column      Non-Null Count  Dtype         
---  ------      --------------  -----         
 0   Month       144 non-null    datetime64[ns]
 1   Passengers  144 non-null    int64         
dtypes: datetime64[ns](1), int64(1)
memory usage: 2.4 KB
In [28]:
df.set_index('Month', inplace=True)
In [29]:
df.isnull().sum()
Out[29]:
Passengers    0
dtype: int64
In [30]:
df = df.dropna()
In [41]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
plt.plot(df)
plt.title("Monthly Airline Passengers")
plt.show()
In [31]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
plt.plot(df)
plt.title("Time Series Data")
plt.xlabel("Month")
plt.ylabel("Value")
plt.show()
In [32]:
result = adfuller(df['Passengers'])

print("ADF Statistic:", result[0])
print("p-value:", result[1])

ADF Statistic: 0.8153688792060511
p-value: 0.991880243437641

Interpretation:

p < 0.05 → Stationary

p > 0.05 → Need differencing

In [33]:
df_diff = df.diff().dropna()

plt.plot(df_diff)
plt.title("Differenced Data")
plt.show()
In [44]:
result = adfuller(df_diff['Passengers'])

print("ADF Statistic:", result[0])
print("p-value:", result[1])

ADF Statistic: -2.8292668241699874
p-value: 0.05421329028382711
In [42]:
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf, plot_pacf
In [43]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))

plot_acf(df_diff['Passengers'], lags=40)

plt.title("Autocorrelation Function (ACF)")
plt.show()

<Figure size 1000x500 with 0 Axes>
In [45]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))

plot_pacf(df_diff['Passengers'], lags=40)

plt.title("Partial Autocorrelation Function (PACF)")
plt.show()

<Figure size 1000x500 with 0 Axes>
In [46]:
train_size = int(len(df) * 0.8)

train = df[:train_size]
test = df[train_size:]
In [53]:
model = ARIMA(train['Passengers'], order=(2,1,5))
model_fit = model.fit()
print(model_fit.summary())

In [59]:
forecast = model_fit.forecast(steps=len(test))

forecast = pd.Series(forecast, index=test.index)
In [60]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))

plt.plot(train, label="Train")
plt.plot(test, label="Actual")
plt.plot(forecast, label="Forecast")

plt.legend()
plt.title("Time Series Forecast")
plt.show()
In [61]:
mae = mean_absolute_error(test, forecast)
rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(test, forecast))

print("MAE:", mae)
print("RMSE:", rmse)

MAE: 59.867858163982234
RMSE: 80.96220237558704
In [57]:
future = model_fit.forecast(steps=30)

print(future)

Forecasting On Air Passenger data¶

In [64]:
df_1=pd.read_csv(r'C:\Users\anupa\Desktop\Python Learning\Forecasting\train.csv')
In [65]:
df_1.head()
Out[65]:
Store Dept Date Weekly_Sales IsHoliday
0 1 1 2010-02-05 24924.50 False
1 1 1 2010-02-12 46039.49 True
2 1 1 2010-02-19 41595.55 False
3 1 1 2010-02-26 19403.54 False
4 1 1 2010-03-05 21827.90 False
In [67]:
df_1['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_1['Date'])
In [68]:
df_1 =df_1.sort_values('Date')
In [69]:
sales = df_1.groupby('Date')['Weekly_Sales'].sum().reset_index()
sales.head()
Out[69]:
Date Weekly_Sales
0 2010-02-05 49750740.50
1 2010-02-12 48336677.63
2 2010-02-19 48276993.78
3 2010-02-26 43968571.13
4 2010-03-05 46871470.30
In [70]:
sales.set_index('Date', inplace=True)
In [82]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
plt.plot(sales)
plt.title("Weekly Retail Sales Trend")
plt.show()
In [71]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))
plt.plot(sales)
plt.title("Walmart Weekly Sales Trend")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("Sales")
plt.show()
In [83]:
sales['RollingMean'] = sales['Weekly_Sales'].rolling(window=12).mean()

sales[['Weekly_Sales','RollingMean']].plot(figsize=(12,6))
Out[83]:
<Axes: xlabel='Date'>
In [84]:
sales['lag_1'] = sales['Weekly_Sales'].shift(1)
sales['lag_2'] = sales['Weekly_Sales'].shift(2)
sales['lag_3'] = sales['Weekly_Sales'].shift(3)

sales.dropna(inplace=True)
In [72]:
train_size = int(len(sales)*0.8)

train = sales[:train_size]
test = sales[train_size:]
In [74]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))

plot_acf(train['Weekly_Sales'], lags=40)

plt.title("Autocorrelation Function (ACF)")
plt.show()

<Figure size 1000x500 with 0 Axes>
In [75]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))

plot_pacf(train['Weekly_Sales'], lags=40)

plt.title("Partial Autocorrelation Function (PACF)")
plt.show()

<Figure size 1000x500 with 0 Axes>
In [76]:
model = ARIMA(train['Weekly_Sales'], order=(5,1,0))
model_fit = model.fit()

print(model_fit.summary())

In [92]:
from pmdarima import auto_arima

model = auto_arima(train,
                   seasonal=True,
                   m=12,
                   trace=True)

In [87]:
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error
from statsmodels.tsa.statespace.sarimax import SARIMAX

#from prophet import Prophet

SARIMA Model¶

In [93]:
#model = SARIMAX(train['Weekly_Sales'],
                #order=(1,1,1),
                #seasonal_order=(1,1,1,12))
model = SARIMAX(train['Weekly_Sales'],
                order=(1,0,0),
                seasonal_order=(0,0,0,12))
result = model.fit()

forecast = result.forecast(steps=len(test))

C:\Users\anupa\anaconda3\Lib\site-packages\statsmodels\tsa\base\tsa_model.py:473: ValueWarning: No frequency information was provided, so inferred frequency W-FRI will be used.

In [94]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))

plt.plot(train.index, train['Weekly_Sales'], label='Train')
plt.plot(test.index, test['Weekly_Sales'], label='Actual')
plt.plot(test.index, forecast, label='Forecast')

plt.legend()
plt.show()
In [ ]:
